comment_text.sn21.3:5.1: Ghaṭā means either “pot” or “mass”. As a measure it is estimated variously at around 10kg, 20kg, or 200kg. Given the context, I think it was a mound of salt such as would be seen at a salt mine.
comment_text.sn21.3:6.1: This verse also found at SN 1.48:4.1, MN 143:17.15, and Thag 20.1:43.1.

root_text.sn21.3:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn21.3:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā ca sāriputto āyasmā ca mahāmoggallāno rājagahe viharanti veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe ekavihāre.
root_text.sn21.3:1.4: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā mahāmoggallānena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn21.3:1.5: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn21.3:2.2: parisuddho mukhavaṇṇo pariyodāto santena nūnāyasmā mahāmoggallāno ajja vihārena vihāsī”ti.
root_text.sn21.3:2.4: Api ca me ahosi dhammī kathā”ti.
root_text.sn21.3:2.5: “Kena saddhiṁ panāyasmato mahāmoggallānassa ahosi dhammī kathā”ti?
root_text.sn21.3:2.6: “Bhagavatā kho me, āvuso, saddhiṁ ahosi dhammī kathā”ti.
root_text.sn21.3:2.7: “Dūre kho, āvuso, bhagavā etarahi sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn21.3:2.8: Kiṁ nu kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno bhagavantaṁ iddhiyā upasaṅkami;
root_text.sn21.3:2.9: udāhu bhagavā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ iddhiyā upasaṅkamī”ti?
root_text.sn21.3:2.12: Api ca me yāvatā bhagavā ettāvatā dibbacakkhu visujjhi dibbā ca sotadhātu.
root_text.sn21.3:2.13: Bhagavatopi yāvatāhaṁ ettāvatā dibbacakkhu visujjhi dibbā ca sotadhātū”ti.
root_text.sn21.3:2.14: “Yathākathaṁ panāyasmato mahāmoggallānassa bhagavatā saddhiṁ ahosi dhammī kathā”ti?
root_text.sn21.3:3.3: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, āraddhavīriyo hotī’ti?
root_text.sn21.3:3.4: Evaṁ vutte, maṁ, āvuso, bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn21.3:3.6: kāmaṁ taco ca nhāru ca aṭṭhi ca avasissatu, sarīre upassussatu maṁsalohitaṁ, yaṁ taṁ purisathāmena purisavīriyena purisaparakkamena pattabbaṁ na taṁ apāpuṇitvā vīriyassa saṇṭhānaṁ bhavissatīti.
root_text.sn21.3:3.8: Evaṁ kho me, āvuso, bhagavatā saddhiṁ ahosi dhammī kathā”ti.
root_text.sn21.3:4.1: “Seyyathāpi, āvuso, himavato pabbatarājassa parittā pāsāṇasakkharā yāvadeva upanikkhepanamattāya;
root_text.sn21.3:4.2: evameva kho mayaṁ āyasmato mahāmoggallānassa yāvadeva upanikkhepanamattāya.
root_text.sn21.3:4.3: Āyasmā hi mahāmoggallāno mahiddhiko mahānubhāvo ākaṅkhamāno kappaṁ tiṭṭheyyā”ti.
root_text.sn21.3:5.1: “Seyyathāpi, āvuso, mahatiyā loṇaghaṭāya parittā loṇasakkharāya yāvadeva upanikkhepanamattāya;
root_text.sn21.3:5.2: evameva kho mayaṁ āyasmato sāriputtassa yāvadeva upanikkhepanamattāya.
root_text.sn21.3:5.3: Āyasmā hi sāriputto bhagavatā anekapariyāyena thomito vaṇṇito pasattho:
root_text.sn21.3:7.1: Itiha te ubho mahānāgā aññamaññassa subhāsitaṁ sulapitaṁ samanumodiṁsūti.

translation_text.sn21.3:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn21.3:1.3: At that time Venerables Sāriputta and Moggallāna were staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn21.3:1.4: Then in the late afternoon, Venerable Sāriputta came out of retreat, went to Venerable Moggallāna, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn21.3:1.5: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, Sāriputta sat down to one side, and said to Mahāmoggallāna:
translation_text.sn21.3:2.1: “Reverend Moggallāna, your faculties are so very clear, and your complexion is pure and bright.
translation_text.sn21.3:2.2: Have you spent the day in a peaceful meditation?”
translation_text.sn21.3:2.3: “Reverend, I’ve spent the day in a coarse meditation.
translation_text.sn21.3:2.4: But I have had some Dhamma talk.”
translation_text.sn21.3:2.5: “Who did you have a Dhamma talk with?”
translation_text.sn21.3:2.7: “But Reverend, the Buddha is far away. He’s staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn21.3:2.8: Did you go to him with your psychic power,
translation_text.sn21.3:2.9: or did he come to you?”
translation_text.sn21.3:2.10: “No reverend, I didn’t go to him with my psychic power,
translation_text.sn21.3:2.11: nor did he come to me.
translation_text.sn21.3:2.12: Rather, the Buddha cleared his clairvoyance and clairaudience towards me,
translation_text.sn21.3:2.13: and I cleared my clairvoyance and clairaudience towards him.”
translation_text.sn21.3:2.14: “But what manner of Dhamma talk did you have together?”
translation_text.sn21.3:3.1: “Well, reverend, I said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn21.3:3.2: ‘Sir, they speak of one who is energetic.
translation_text.sn21.3:3.3: How is an energetic person defined?’
translation_text.sn21.3:3.4: When I said this, the Buddha said,
translation_text.sn21.3:3.5: ‘Moggallāna, it’s when a mendicant lives with energy roused up:
translation_text.sn21.3:3.6: “Gladly, let only skin, sinews, and bones remain! Let the flesh and blood waste away in my body! I will not stop trying until I have achieved what is possible by human strength, energy, and vigor.”
translation_text.sn21.3:3.7: That’s how an individual is energetic.’
translation_text.sn21.3:3.8: That’s the Dhamma talk I had together with the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn21.3:4.1: “Reverend, next to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna I’m like a few pieces of gravel next to the Himalayas, the king of mountains.
translation_text.sn21.3:4.3: Venerable Mahāmoggallāna is so mighty and powerful he could, if he wished, live on for the proper lifespan.”
translation_text.sn21.3:5.1: “Reverend, next to Venerable Sāriputta I’m like a few grains of salt next to a mound of salt.
translation_text.sn21.3:5.3: Venerable Sāriputta has been commended, complimented, and praised by the Buddha:
translation_text.sn21.3:6.3: Even a mendicant who has crossed over
translation_text.sn21.3:7.1: And so these two spiritual giants agreed with each others’ fine words.
